title: Implement dual-context Pi setup (personal + work)
description:
Support two separate Pi agent dirs: ~/.config/pi (personal) and ~/.config/pi-work (work/amfaro). Includes:
  - PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR environment variable support for work context (via mise)
  - Refactored auto-caveman extension to load all caveman skills and patch to ultra mode
  - New auto-work extension for work-only context
  - Separate settings files for personal/work with shared extensions/themes
  - pi-work symlinks back to personal themes dir to avoid duplication
close_reason: Completed: dual-context Pi setup with personal/work dirs, refactored extensions, and separate settings

title: jarify: lint violations always report line=None/column=None
description: Rules access sqlglot node position via getattr(node, 'line', None) but sqlglot stores position in node.meta — e.g. node.meta.get('line'). Fix: use node.meta.get('line') / node.meta.get('col') in all rule check() methods. Once fixed, jarify lint --format json will emit accurate positions, enabling proper inline diagnostics in editors.
close_reason: Fixed in amfaro/jarify#66

title: Fix LSP circular require after Neovim 0.11 upgrade
description: Neovim 0.11 changed vim.lsp.enable() to eagerly load lsp/*.lua files at call time. Since config/lsp.lua called vim.lsp.enable() during module load, and each lsp/*.lua required config.lsp back, Lua detected a circular dependency and errored. Fixed by moving on_attach definition before vim.lsp.enable, using vim.lsp.config() for global attach, and removing per-file requires.
close_reason: Fixed by restructuring config/lsp.lua: moved on_attach before vim.lsp.enable, added vim.lsp.config('*', {on_attach=...}) for global attach, removed require('config.lsp') from all lsp/*.lua files, and uncommented/fixed basedpyright.lua with inlined shared keymaps.

title: Fix LSP circular require after Neovim 0.11 upgrade
description: Neovim 0.11 changed vim.lsp.enable() to eagerly load lsp/*.lua files at call time. Since config/lsp.lua called vim.lsp.enable() during module load, and each lsp/*.lua required config.lsp back, Lua detected a circular dependency and errored. Fixed by moving on_attach definition before vim.lsp.enable, using vim.lsp.config('*', {on_attach=...}) for global attach, and removing per-file requires.

nix-rebuild failing due to Swift 5.10.1 build failure on darwin. Root cause: feishin → mpv-with-scripts → mpv → swift dependency chain. Fix: Remove feishin from nix packages (no longer used).

Upstream issue: https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/issues/483584
close_reason: Removed feishin from packages/default.nix. Build now succeeds with latest nixpkgs.

title: mpv-updater: repeat indicator not responding to L key toggle
description:
## Problem
The repeat indicator in bottombar always showed "on" even when toggling repeat with the L key in mpv.

## Root Cause
The mpv-updater script was only checking `loop-playlist` property, but mpv's default 'L' keybinding toggles `loop-file` (single track repeat), not `loop-playlist`.

## Solution
Updated the script to check both `loop-file` and `loop-playlist` properties. The repeat indicator now shows when either property is set to "inf".

## Files Changed
- `modules/home-manager/dotfiles/config/bottombar/plugins/mpv-updater`
close_reason: fixed

title: Fix bottombar not displaying MPV music info - PATH issue in mpv-updater script
description: The bottombar was not displaying any music info from MPV. Investigation revealed that the mpv-updater script was failing because commands like 'bottombar', 'jq', 'socat', and 'ffmpeg' were not in the PATH when launched from the Lua sketchybarrc. The fix adds an explicit PATH export at the top of the mpv-updater script to include nix-installed binaries.

title: Investigate MPV playback mode for elapsed time via IPC socket
description:
Feishin can use MPV for playback instead of web player. MPV exposes an IPC socket that can be queried for time-pos (elapsed time). If feishin creates an MPV socket when using MPV playback mode, we could query it from the bottombar plugin to display elapsed/total time.

Steps:
1. Switch feishin to MPV playback mode in settings
2. Check if an MPV IPC socket is created (look in /tmp or feishin config dir)
3. If socket exists, query it with: echo '{"command": ["get_property", "time-pos"]}' | socat - /path/to/socket
4. If working, update feishin-updater script to poll the socket for time info

Related: system-config-1e4 (bottombar feishin plugin)

title: Add restart-bars script for launchd services
description: After nix-rebuild, launchd plist reloads can leave UI services (borders, sketchybar, bottombar) in a non-functional state. A force-restart script using 'launchctl kickstart -k' would be useful for quick recovery. Question: mise task vs home-manager bin script (globally available from anywhere vs only in repo). Current manual fix: launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/org.nixos.<service> for borders, sketchybar, bottombar.
